goal: Build a /smoke-test skill that runs a real directive through the pipeline, auto-approving at all gates, and reports pass/fail per step

risks: Real pipeline execution is non-deterministic — LLM agents may stall, produce unexpected output, or hit context limits. The smoke test needs robust timeout handling per step and clear failure reporting.

Test isolation via branch risks polluting the real directive index. The dashboard directive-watcher reads .context/directives/ via chokidar — test directives will appear in the live dashboard during execution.
The completion gate test_mode auto-approve creates a path where a real directive could accidentally skip CEO review if directive.json is hand-edited.
over_engineering_flags: Do NOT build a custom test framework or assertion library. A single bash script that spawns one directive session, polls directive.json, and validates artifacts is sufficient.
Only move the 2 pipeline-mechanical scenarios. Do NOT rewrite or expand them.

projects.title: Build /smoke-test skill with real pipeline execution
projects.scope_summary: Create .claude/skills/smoke-test/SKILL.md that orchestrates end-to-end pipeline smoke testing. Build a runner script (bash) that creates a test directive, spawns a real /directive session, polls directive.json for progression, validates artifacts via validate-gate.sh, auto-approves at completion via test_mode flag, reports pass/fail per step, and cleans up. Patch 11-completion-gate.md for test_mode. Migrate 2 pipeline-mechanical scenarios from lessons/scenarios.md.
